---
title: "FORWARDS_pre_analysis"
author: "Annemarie Eckes-Shephard"
date: "2025-10-29"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}

#devtools::install_github("MagicForrest/DGVMTools")
library(DGVMTools)
library(gridExtra)
base_dir = "/Volumes/Anne's Backup/ForestPaths/v16/"
source("~/Documents/1_TreeMort/2_Analysis/3_analysis_ForestPaths_Exploratory_runs/scripts/helper_functions.R")
country="Sweden" #"Sweden" # "Germany", "Poland", "Spain"

base_dir <- "/Volumes/Anne's Backup/FORWARDS/"

plot_all = FALSE

var = "cmass_forest"

gridlist <- read.table("../../3_analysis_ForestPaths_Exploratory_runs/scripts/gridlist_final.txt")
plot(gridlist$V1,gridlist$V2)


points( 28.25, 64.25,col="red",pch=16)
#        25.25, 61.75
# we use management names, and ForestPaths uses cases ( numbers). here I can quickly map one to the other:
# from Table 2b in Exploratory Cases M7.
# 999 means these are not actually part of ForestPaths
cases=data.frame( man=c("base","lightthin", "intensthin","longrot", "shortrot", "rettree", "sfire", "fertfor","ceaseman","ccf"), 
                  FPcase = c(0  ,6,                    7  , 8      ,      9    ,    10    ,   11  ,     12   ,    14    , 999) )


#also create the correct expression for Scenario:
scenarios = data.frame(sc   = c("hist","ssp126","ssp370"),
                       FPsc = c("hist","SSP1-RCP2.6","SSP3-RCP7.0"))

landcover_fractions_from_2025 <- read.table("../../3_analysis_ForestPaths_Exploratory_runs/notes/landcover_fractions_from_2025.txt",head=TRUE)
forestcover_fractions_from_2025 <- read.table("../../3_analysis_ForestPaths_Exploratory_runs/notes/forest_cover_fractions_from_2025.txt",head=TRUE)

#create plotting specs dataframe
# Define vectors
managementslist <- c("base","fertfor","lightthin","intensthin","longrot",
                     "shortrot","rettree","ceaseman","sfire","ccf","ceaseman_cct","ceaseman_cct_distoff","divers","ccf_divers")

mancol <- c(1:8, "brown", "darkgreen", "purple","purple","magenta")
manpch <- c(1, 2, 3, 3, 4, 4, 5, 5,5, 5, 5, 1,6)
manlty <- manpch

# Combine into a data frame
man <- data.frame(
  management = managementslist,
  col = mancol[1:length(managementslist)],
  pch = manpch[1:length(managementslist)],
  lty = manlty[1:length(managementslist)],
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Management scenarios (excluding fertfor, ceaseman, ceaseman_cct)
managements_list <- c("base", "lightthin", "intensthin", "longrot",
                      "shortrot", "rettree", "sfire", "ccf", "divers","ceaseman", "ceaseman_cct", "ccf_divers","fertfor")#,"ccf")#
managementslist =  managements_list
man <- management_df <- data.frame(
  management = managements_list,
  col = c(
    "black",    # base
    "#66BB6A",  # lightthin    — medium green   } thinning pair
    "#1B5E20",  # intensthin   — dark green     }
    "#64B5F6",  # longrot      — light blue     } rotation pair
    "#0D47A1",  # shortrot     — dark blue      }
    "#7030A0",  # rettree      — purple
    "#973735",  # sfire        — dark red
    "#FBA918",  # ccf          — amber          } ccf pair
    "#00897B",  # divers       — teal
    "#3E4FC2",  # ceaseman     — indigo         } ceaseman pair
    "#1A237E",  # ceaseman_cct — dark navy      }
    "#E65100",  # ccf_divers   — deep orange    }
    "grey"      # fertilise forest
  ),
  pch = c(1, 3, 3, 4, 4, 5, 7, 8, 15, 16, 17, 18,1),
  lty = c(1, 3, 3, 4, 4, 5, 2, 6,  2,  5,  5,  6,1),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)


```

```{r ceaseman_cct_fut_MPI-ESM1-2-HR_ssp126_diston_document failed gridcells}

#Some cells failed timed out, but only few, and interestingly all on the sam lat.
#Unfinished gridcells found in the following runs:
# run13 (4 unfinished)
#lon.   lat
21.25 42.25
21.75 42.25
22.25 42.25
22.75 42.25

# run15 (8 unfinished)
22.75 42.75
23.25 42.75
23.75 42.75
24.25 42.75
24.75 42.75
25.25 42.75
25.75 42.75
26.25 42.75

# run16 (2 unfinished)
2.75 43.25
3.25 43.25

# run17 (1 unfinished)
26.25 43.25


data_unfinished <- data.frame(
  Lon = c(21.25, 21.75, 22.25, 22.75, 22.75, 23.25, 23.75, 24.25, 24.75, 25.25, 25.75, 26.25, 2.75, 3.25, 26.25),
  Lat = c(42.25, 42.25, 42.25, 42.25, 42.75, 42.75, 42.75, 42.75, 42.75, 42.75, 42.75, 42.75, 43.25, 43.25, 43.25),
  run_number = c(NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA)
)


gridlist <- read.table("../../3_analysis_ForestPaths_Exploratory_runs/european_applications/testrun/gridlist_final.txt")
plot(gridlist$V1,gridlist$V2, main="unfinished Gridcells")

points(data_unfinished$Lon,data_unfinished$Lat,col="red")

```


```{r ceaseman_cct_fut_MPI-ESM1-2-HR_ssp126_diston}

management = "ceaseman_cct"
scenario = "ssp126" #"ssp126"
disturbance = "diston" # diston


source.in_ceaseman_cct = defineSource(id= paste0(management,"_",scenario),
              dir= paste0(base_dir,management,"_fut_MPI-ESM1-2-HR_",scenario,"_",disturbance,"/"),#,management,"_fut_MPI-ESM1-2-HR_",scenario,"_",disturbance,"_2025-04-07/"),
             format = GUESS,
             name = paste0(management,"_",scenario))


#first visualisations for output:
myData <- getField(source = source.in_ceaseman_cct,quant="cmass_forest")
plotSpatial(myData,year=c(2025,2030,2050,2070,2100),layers = "Total",limit=c(0,22))




base_dir_FP  <- "/Volumes/Anne's Backup/v13/"
management = "ceaseman"
scenario = "ssp126" #"ssp126"
disturbance = "diston" # diston
source.in_ceaseman = defineSource(id= paste0(management,"_",scenario),
              dir= paste0(base_dir_FP,management,"_fut_MPI-ESM1-2-HR_",scenario,"_",disturbance,"/"),#,management,"_fut_MPI-ESM1-2-HR_",scenario,"_",disturbance,"_2025-04-07/"),
             format = GUESS,
             name = paste0(management,"_",scenario))


#first visualisations for output:
myData <- getField(source = source.in_ceaseman,quant="cmass_forest")
plotSpatial(myData,year=c(2025,2030,2050,2070,2100),layers = "Total",limit=c(0,22))



management = "ccf"
scenario = "ssp126" #"ssp126"
disturbance = "diston" # diston
source.in_ccf = defineSource(id= paste0(management,"_",scenario),
              dir= paste0(base_dir_FP,management,"_fut_MPI-ESM1-2-HR_",scenario,"_",disturbance,"/"),#,management,"_fut_MPI-ESM1-2-HR_",scenario,"_",disturbance,"_2025-04-07/"),
             format = GUESS,
             name = paste0(management,"_",scenario))


#first visualisations for output:
myData <- getField(source = source.in_ccf,quant="cmass_forest")
plotSpatial(myData,year=c(2025,2030,2050,2070,2100),layers = "Total",limit=c(0,22))


management = "rettree"
scenario = "ssp126" #"ssp126"
disturbance = "diston" # diston
source.in_rettree = defineSource(id= paste0(management,"_",scenario),
              dir= paste0(base_dir_FP,management,"_fut_MPI-ESM1-2-HR_",scenario,"_",disturbance,"/"),#,management,"_fut_MPI-ESM1-2-HR_",scenario,"_",disturbance,"_2025-04-07/"),
             format = GUESS,
             name = paste0(management,"_",scenario))


#first visualisations for output:
myData <- getField(source = source.in_rettree,quant="cmass_forest")
plotSpatial(myData,year=c(2025,2030,2050,2070,2100),layers = "Total",limit=c(0,22))


management = "base"
scenario = "ssp126" #"ssp126"
disturbance = "diston" # diston
source.in_baseline = defineSource(id= paste0(management,"_",scenario),
              dir= paste0(base_dir_FP,management,"_fut_MPI-ESM1-2-HR_",scenario,"_",disturbance,"/"),#,management,"_fut_MPI-ESM1-2-HR_",scenario,"_",disturbance,"_2025-04-07/"),
             format = GUESS,
             name = paste0(management,"_",scenario))


#first visualisations for output:
myData <- getField(source = source.in_baseline,quant="cmass_forest")
plotSpatial(myData,year=c(2025,2030,2050,2070,2100),layers = "Total",limit=c(0,22))


management = "sfire"
scenario = "ssp126" #"ssp126"
disturbance = "diston" # diston
source.in_sfire = defineSource(id= paste0(management,"_",scenario),
              dir= paste0(base_dir_FP,management,"_fut_MPI-ESM1-2-HR_",scenario,"_",disturbance,"/"),#,management,"_fut_MPI-ESM1-2-HR_",scenario,"_",disturbance,"_2025-04-07/"),
             format = GUESS,
             name = paste0(management,"_",scenario))


#first visualisations for output:
myData <- getField(source = source.in_sfire,quant="cmass_forest")
plotSpatial(myData,year=c(2025,2030,2050,2070,2100),layers = "Total",limit=c(0,22))

##gini:

myData_source.in_ceaseman_cct <- calculate_gini(source_name = source.in_ceaseman_cct)
myData.source.in_ceaseman <- calculate_gini(source_name = source.in_ceaseman)
myData.source.in_rettree <- calculate_gini(source_name = source.in_rettree,management_name ="rettree")
myData.source.in_ccf <- calculate_gini(source_name = source.in_ccf)
myData.source.in_baseline <- calculate_gini(source_name = source.in_baseline)
myData.source.sfire <- calculate_gini(source_name = source.in_sfire,management_name ="sfire")


plotSpatial(myData_source.in_ceaseman_cct,year=c(2025,2030,2050,2070,2100),layers = "Forest_sum",limits=c(0,0.7))
plotSpatial(myData.source.in_ceaseman,year=c(2025,2030,2050,2070,2100),layers = "Forest_sum",limits=c(0,0.7))
plotSpatial(myData.source.in_rettree,year=c(2025,2030,2050,2070,2100),layers = "Forest_sum",limits=c(0,0.7))
plotSpatial(myData.source.in_ccf,year=c(2025,2030,2050,2070,2100),layers = "Forest_sum",limits=c(0,0.7))
plotSpatial(myData.source.in_baseline,year=c(2025,2030,2050,2070,2100),layers = "Forest_sum",limits=c(0,0.7))
plotSpatial(myData.source.sfire,year=c(2025,2030,2050,2070,2100),layers = "Forest_sum",limits=c(0,0.7))

```

```{r for Milestone reports_ replicating Mart_Jans plot structure}
# milestone report run with v13.
# switched to v16 - after recalibration

landcover_fractions =   read.table("../../3_analysis_ForestPaths_Exploratory_runs/landuse_change_forest_age/lu_inputfiles_noNF3/net_lu_HildaPucherMircaEurope.txt", head=TRUE)
landcover_fractions <- landcover_fractions[which(landcover_fractions$Year>=2025),]# subset, landcover constent from 2025.


###  ABSOLUTE" ###############
#pool
managementslist <- c("base","fertfor","lightthin", "intensthin","longrot", "shortrot","rettree" ,"ceaseman","sfire","ccf","ceaseman_cct","ceaseman_cct_distoff","divers")

#for plotting:
i=2
scenariolist <- c("ssp126")
countrylist = c("Sweden","Finland","Germany","Poland","France","Netherlands","Spain","Italy")
extract_c=FALSE
dist= "diston"

if(extract_c){
managementslist <- c("base","fertfor","lightthin","intensthin","longrot",
                     "shortrot","rettree","sfire","ccf","divers")
}else{
  managementslist <- c("base","fertfor","lightthin","intensthin","longrot",
                     "shortrot","rettree","ceaseman","sfire","ccf","ceaseman_cct","ceaseman_cct_distoff","divers")
}

if(extract_c){
par(mfrow=c(2,4), mar=c(1,1,3,0), oma=c(3,4,1,0))
  
  for(country in countrylist){
    for(management in managementslist){
      
      if(management == "ceaseman_cct_distoff"){
        management = "ceaseman_cct"
        dist = "distoff"
      }else{
        dist ="diston"
      }
      
      if(management =="ceaseman_ccst"){
    base_dir = "/Volumes/Anne's Backup/FORWARDS/"
  }else{
    base_dir = "/Volumes/Anne's Backup/ForestPaths/v16/"
  }
      for(scenario in scenariolist){
        
        if(dist == "distoff"){
          folder_name <- paste0(base_dir,management, "_fut_MPI-ESM1-2-HR_",
                                scenario, "_distoff")
        }else{
          folder_name <- paste0(base_dir,management, "_fut_MPI-ESM1-2-HR_",
                                scenario, "_diston")
        }
        
        #SSP3-RCP7.0_10_LPJ-GUESS.csv
        #retrieve the source file location + add some metadata:
        source.in = defineSource(id= paste0(management,"_",scenario),
                                 dir= paste0(folder_name),
                                 format = GUESS,
                                 name = paste0(management,"_",scenario))
        
        
        
        myData <- getField(source = source.in, quant = "diam_cmass_wood")
        myData@data$Total  <-  rowSums(myData@data[,4:dim(myData@data)[2]])
        
       
        myData <- extract_country(myData, input_name = country, path_to_gridlist_with_country_names ="../../3_analysis_ForestPaths_Exploratory_runs/scripts/gridlist_final.txt_country_names_added" )
        ###Calculate area-weighted mean:
        # Add forest area
        myData@data <- myData@data[landcover_fractions[,c("Lon","Lat","Year","FOREST")], on = .(Lon, Lat, Year)]
        myData@data$GridcellArea_m2 <- GridcellArea_m2(myData@data$Lat,res=0.5)
        myData@data$Forest_area <- myData@data$GridcellArea_m2 * myData@data$FOREST
    
         # compute mean across years for each Lon, Lat
        myData_agg <- myData
        myData_agg@data <- myData@data[, lapply(.SD, function(x) weighted.mean(x, w = Forest_area, na.rm = TRUE)), 
                                                   by = .(Year), 
                                                   .SDcols = "Total"]
        
        if(management == "base"){
          base_agg <- myData_agg 
        }
        #myData_agg_GA <- GridcellArea_m2(myData_agg)
        # for baseline on off: look at cmass, look at NBP
        myData_agg@data$Total = myData_agg@data$Total - base_agg@data$Total
        
        
        if(management =="base"){
          plot(myData_agg@data$Year,myData_agg@data$Total,col=man[man$man == management, ]$col
,type= "l", ylim=c( -0.5,1), lwd=2,
               ylab=c("difference in Carbon in biomass, kgC/m2"),main=paste(country))
        }else{
          if(management=="base_off"){
            lines(myData_agg@data$Year,myData_agg@data$Total,col=man[man$man == management, ]$col
,lty= man[man$man == management, ]$lty)
          }else{
            lines(myData_agg@data$Year,myData_agg@data$Total,col=man[man$man == management, ]$col
,lty= man[man$man == management, ]$lty)
           
          }
          
        }
        
      }
      
    }
  }
}else{
    par(mfrow=c(1,1))
    for(management in managementslist){
      
            if(management == "ceaseman_cct_distoff"){
        management= "ceaseman_cct"
        dist = "distoff"
      }else{
        dist ="diston"
      }
      
      if(management =="ceaseman_csct"){
    base_dir = "/Volumes/Anne's Backup/FORWARDS/"
  }else{
    base_dir =  "/Volumes/Anne's Backup/ForestPaths/v16/"
  }
      for(scenario in scenariolist){
        
        if(dist == "distoff"){
          folder_name <- paste0(base_dir,management, "_fut_MPI-ESM1-2-HR_",
                                scenario, "_distoff")
        }else{
          folder_name <- paste0(base_dir,management, "_fut_MPI-ESM1-2-HR_",
                                scenario, "_diston")
        }
        #SSP3-RCP7.0_10_LPJ-GUESS.csv
        #retrieve the source file location + add some metadata:
        source.in = defineSource(id= paste0(management,"_",scenario),
                                 dir= paste0(folder_name),
                                 format = GUESS,
                                 name = paste0(management,"_",scenario))
        
        
        
        myData <- getField(source = source.in, quant = "diam_cmass_wood")
        myData@data$Total  <-  rowSums(myData@data[,4:dim(myData@data)[2]])
        
        
        ###Calculate area-weighted mean:
        # Add forest area
        myData@data <- myData@data[landcover_fractions[,c("Lon","Lat","Year","FOREST")], on = .(Lon, Lat, Year)]
        myData@data$GridcellArea_m2 <- GridcellArea_m2(myData@data$Lat,res=0.5)
        myData@data$Forest_area <- myData@data$GridcellArea_m2 * myData@data$FOREST
    
        # compute mean across years for each Lon, Lat
        myData_agg <- myData
        myData_agg@data <- myData@data[, lapply(.SD, function(x) weighted.mean(x, w = Forest_area, na.rm = TRUE)), 
                                                   by = .(Year), 
                                                   .SDcols = "Total"]
    
        #myData_agg <- aggregateSpatial(myData,method="mean")
            
        
      
        #if(management == "base"){
        #  base_agg <- myData_agg 
        #}
        #myData_agg_GA <- GridcellArea_m2(myData_agg)
        # for baseline on off: look at cmass, look at NBP
        #myData_agg@data$Total = myData_agg@data$Total - base_agg@data$Total
        
        
        if(management =="base"){
          plot(myData_agg@data$Year,myData_agg@data$Total,col=man[man$man == management, ]$col
,type= "l", ylim=c( 0,8.5), lwd=2,
               ylab=c("veg carbon in biomass, kgC/m2"),main=paste(scenario))
        }else{
          if(management=="base_off"){
            lines(myData_agg@data$Year,myData_agg@data$Total,col=man[man$man == management, ]$col
,lty= man[man$man == management, ]$lty)
          }else{
            lines(myData_agg@data$Year,myData_agg@data$Total,col=man[man$man == management, ]$col
,lty= man[man$man == management, ]$lty)
            
          }
          
        }
        
      }
    }
}
    
#    legend(legend=c(managementslist), fill=c(2:length(managementslist)), "topleft",cex=0.8)
   # plot.new()
 # legend(legend=c(managementslist), fill = man$col, lty = man$pch, "bottomright",cex=0.8)

if(extract_c){
  mtext(outer=TRUE,side=2,"differene in vegetation biomass (kgC/m2)", line=2)
  mtext(outer=TRUE,side=1,"Year",1.5)
}else{
  legend(legend=c(managementslist), fill = man$col, lty = man$pch, "bottomright",cex=0.6)
   mtext(outer=TRUE,side=2,"vegetation biomass (kgC/m2)", line=2)
}

  
    
```



```{r for Risk_analysis}

v16 <- "/Volumes/Anne's Backup/ForestPaths/v16/"  
base_dir <- v16
source("../../3_analysis_ForestPaths_Exploratory_runs/scripts/helper_functions.R")
management ="base"
scenarios=c("ssp126","ssp370")

landcover_fractions =   read.table("../../3_analysis_ForestPaths_Exploratory_runs/landuse_change_forest_age/lu_inputfiles_noNF3/net_lu_HildaPucherMircaEurope.txt", head=TRUE)
landcover_fractions <- landcover_fractions[which(landcover_fractions$Year>=2025),]# subset, landcover constent from 2025.

for(scenario in scenarios){
#retrieve the source file location + add some metadata:
  folder_name <- paste0(base_dir,management, "_fut_MPI-ESM1-2-HR_",
                        scenario, "_diston")
  source.in = defineSource(id= paste0(management,"_",scenario),
                           dir= paste0(folder_name),
                           format = GUESS,
                           name = paste0(management,"_",scenario))
  
  
    # 1. Get forest biomass (from cmass_wood_sts or diam_cmass_wood)
    myData_cmass <- getField(source = source.in, quant = "cmass_wood_sts")

    
    # 2. Get biomass lost through disturbance
    # at gridcell-level. To get Forest -only wind_losses: /forest fraction.
    cmass_wood_lost <- getField(source = source.in, quant = "storm")
    
     # Add forest area
     cmass_wood_lost@data <- cmass_wood_lost@data[landcover_fractions[,c("Lon","Lat","Year","FOREST")], on = .(Lon, Lat, Year)]
     cmass_wood_lost@data$GridcellArea_m2 <- GridcellArea_m2(cmass_wood_lost@data$Lat, res = 0.5)
     cmass_wood_lost@data$Forest_area <- cmass_wood_lost@data$GridcellArea_m2 * cmass_wood_lost@data$FOREST
  
    # what level are we for biomass and for disturbance losses? 
    # I want the output to be on forest - area, not gridcell area, so per m2 forest.
    cmass_wood_lost@data$Forest_Insect_damage <- cmass_wood_lost@data$InsDWC /  cmass_wood_lost@data$FOREST
    cmass_wood_lost@data$Forest_Storm_damage  <- cmass_wood_lost@data$DamWoodC /  cmass_wood_lost@data$FOREST
    
    #merge forest biomass and biomass loss tables: 
    ss <- cmass_wood_lost@data[ myData_cmass@data[,c("Forest_sum","Lat","Lon","Year")],  on = .(Lon, Lat, Year)]
    ss$Forest_Insect_damage_frac <- ss$Forest_Insect_damage / ss$Forest_sum 
    ss$Forest_Storm_damage_frac  <- ss$Forest_Storm_damage / ss$Forest_sum 
    ss <- ss[which(ss$Year>2024),]
    
    #calculate the cumulative sum of Insect and Storm damage fractions for the timeperiods 2025-2050, 2050-2070, 2070-2100
    
    library(data.table)

    # Define time period breaks; have the same period lengths: 
    ss[, Year := fcase(
      Year >= 2030 & Year <= 2050, as.numeric("2050"),
      Year > 2051  & Year <= 2070, as.numeric("2070"),
      Year > 2071  & Year <= 2090, as.numeric("2090")
    )]
    
    # Cumulative sums by gridcell and period
    cumsum_damage <- ss[
      !is.na(Year),
      .(
        CumInsect = sum(Forest_Insect_damage_frac, na.rm = TRUE),
        CumStorm  = sum(Forest_Storm_damage_frac,  na.rm = TRUE),
        FOREST    = first(FOREST)
      ),
      by = .(Lon, Lat, Year)
    ]
    
    cumsum_damage <- cumsum_damage[which(!is.infinite(cumsum_damage$CumStorm)),]
    cmass_wood_lost@data <- cumsum_damage
    plotSpatial(cmass_wood_lost, years=c(2050,2070,2090),layers="CumInsect", legend.title = "Insect damage fraction")
    plotSpatial(cmass_wood_lost, years=c(2050,2070,2090),layers="CumStorm", legend.title = "storm damage fraction")
    
    
    print(scenario)
    print(summary(cumsum_damage))
    print(quantile(cumsum_damage$CumStorm,0.75))
    print(quantile(cumsum_damage$CumInsect,0.75))
    if(scenario=="ssp126"){
          write.csv(cumsum_damage, "../data/processed/ssp126_disturbance_risk.csv", row.names = FALSE)
        }else{
          write.csv(cumsum_damage, "../data/processed/ssp370_disturbance_risk.csv", row.names = FALSE)
        }
    
}
```




```{r choose_regions}

probharv_cluster <- read.table("/Users/annemarie/Documents/1_TreeMort/2_Analysis/3_analysis_ForestPaths_Exploratory_runs/Probabilistic_harvest/lpjg_inputfiles_extrapolate_fmapOnly6_041224/p_harvest_Conifer.txt",header = TRUE)
probharv_cluster$cluster <- rowSums(probharv_cluster[,3:dim(probharv_cluster)[2]])

plot(probharv_cluster$lon,probharv_cluster$lat,col =probharv_cluster$cluster)

probharv_cluster <- read.table("/Users/annemarie/Documents/1_TreeMort/2_Analysis/3_analysis_ForestPaths_Exploratory_runs/Probabilistic_harvest/lpjg_inputfiles_extrapolate_fmapOnly6_041224/p_harvest_Broadleaf.txt",header = TRUE)
probharv_cluster$cluster <- rowSums(probharv_cluster[,3:dim(probharv_cluster)[2]])

plot(probharv_cluster$lon,probharv_cluster$lat,col =probharv_cluster$cluster)
names(probharv_cluster)[1:2] <- c("Lon","Lat") # rename for better merginf later

# choose gridlists:
# some with dominant vegetation characteristic of that region? otherwise, if we do a gridcell of each vegetation /stand type, we would bias the results I think.
# Cross the harvest clusters with dominant PFT maybe?

# create groups by dominant stand types + harvest cluster, group Fagus and otherBL together. 
stand_types  <-  read.table("/Users/annemarie/Documents/1_TreeMort/2_Analysis/3_analysis_ForestPaths_Exploratory_runs/landuse_change_forest_age/lu_inputfiles_noNF3_corrected/luforest_HildaPucherMircaEurope.txt",header = TRUE)

# Land use constant, so can extract just one year:
stand_types <- stand_types[which(stand_types$Year==2025),]
# add Fagus to other BL,
stand_types$otherBL <- stand_types$otherBL+stand_types$Fagus 
stand_types$Fagus <- NULL

#also remove Lariz and otherNL, they are not extensive anough and fractional cover is too low (eyeballing)
stand_types$Larix <- NULL
stand_types$otherNL <- NULL

# Now we have Quercus, otherBL, Pinus, Picea clusters

library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

# Reshape data to long format
stand_types_long <- stand_types %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = Picea:otherBL, names_to = "Genus", values_to = "Fractional_Cover")

# Plot maps
ggplot(stand_types_long, aes(x = Lon, y = Lat, fill = Fractional_Cover)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "plasma", name = "Fractional Cover") + # Color scale
  facet_wrap(~Genus, ncol = 4) + # Arrange maps in a grid
  coord_fixed() + # Ensure correct aspect ratio
  labs(title = "Fractional Cover of Genera", x = "Longitude", y = "Latitude") +
  theme_minimal()

# Only keep the dominant stand type
stand_types_long_subs <- stand_types_long %>% group_by(Lon,Lat,Year) %>% mutate(Max_Cover=max(Fractional_Cover))
stand_types_long_subs <- stand_types_long_subs[which(stand_types_long_subs$Fractional_Cover == stand_types_long_subs$Max_Cover),]

stand_types_long_subs <- as.data.table(stand_types_long_subs)
probharv_cluster <- as.data.table(probharv_cluster)
## overlay the two maps 
harv_st_clusters <- stand_types_long_subs[probharv_cluster[,c("Lon","Lat","cluster")], on = .(Lon,Lat)]

# seems I am working with a stand type map which does not fully overlao with the proharvest map, but that's no problem here. just omitting the NA rows:
harv_st_clusters <- harv_st_clusters[which(!is.na(harv_st_clusters$Year)),]
harv_st_clusters$NatForest <- NULL

# Maybe preferentially use gridcells within a cluster that  have a fractional cover of a stand that is > 0.5.
harv_st_clusters_subs <- harv_st_clusters[which(harv_st_clusters$Max_Cover > 0.45),]
plot(harv_st_clusters_subs$Lon,harv_st_clusters_subs$Lat,col=harv_st_clusters_subs$cluster)

# Manually rename the clusters of interest to numbers 1-7 to correspond to Susanna's clusters.
#First, assign some number to avoid the annoying floating point comparison:
cluster_key <- data.table(
  cluster = unique(harv_st_clusters_subs$cluster),
  cluster_id = seq_along(unique(harv_st_clusters_subs$cluster))
)

harv_st_clusters_subs[cluster_key, on = "cluster", cluster_id := i.cluster_id]
plot(harv_st_clusters_subs$Lon,harv_st_clusters_subs$Lat,col=harv_st_clusters_subs$cluster_id)
legend(legend=c(unique(harv_st_clusters_subs$cluster_id)),fill=unique(harv_st_clusters_subs$cluster_id),"bottomleft",cex=0.5)
harv_st_clusters_subs$cluster_probharv <- as.numeric(0)

#MANUALLY ASSIGN SUSANNAS CLUSTER NUMBERS:
#harv_st_clusters_subs[which(harv_st_clusters_subs$cluster_id == 1) ,]$cluster_probharv <- 7.0
#harv_st_clusters_subs[which(harv_st_clusters_subs$cluster_id == 1) ,]$cluster_probharv <- 7.0
#skipping this..

harv_st_clusters_subs[, Group := NA_character_]
#harv_st_clusters_subs$Group <- NULL# as.character()

#Group1: otherBL + probharv cluster 7:
harv_st_clusters_subs[which(harv_st_clusters_subs$Genus == "otherBL" & harv_st_clusters_subs$cluster_id ==18 & harv_st_clusters_subs$Lon >= 20 & harv_st_clusters_subs$Lat <= 55),]$Group <- "EasternEurope"

harv_st_clusters_subs[which(harv_st_clusters_subs$Genus == "otherBL" & harv_st_clusters_subs$cluster_id ==18 & harv_st_clusters_subs$Lon <= 20 & harv_st_clusters_subs$Lat >= 50),]$Group <- "CentralEuropeBL"


#Group2: Pinus + probharv cluster 2:
harv_st_clusters_subs[which( harv_st_clusters_subs$cluster_id == 25 &  harv_st_clusters_subs$Lon <= 20),]$Group   <- "SouthernSweden"


#Group3: Quercus + probharv cluster 1:
harv_st_clusters_subs[which((harv_st_clusters_subs$cluster_id == 25 |   harv_st_clusters_subs$cluster_id == 32 ) & harv_st_clusters_subs$Lon >= 20),]$Group  <- "BalticsSouthernFinland"
#harv_st_clusters_subs$Lon >= 20 & harv_st_clusters_subs$Lon <= 0),]$Group  <- "Baltics"


#Group4: Picea + probharv cluster 7:
harv_st_clusters_subs[which(harv_st_clusters_subs$Genus == "Picea" & harv_st_clusters_subs$cluster_id == 18),]$Group    <- "CentralEuropeNL"


#Group5: Picea + probharv cluster 3:
harv_st_clusters_subs[which(harv_st_clusters_subs$Genus == "Pinus" & harv_st_clusters_subs$cluster_id == 31 & harv_st_clusters_subs$Lat >= 58),]$Group   <- "NorthernFinland" 

#Group6: Picea + probharv cluster 3:
harv_st_clusters_subs[which(harv_st_clusters_subs$Genus == "Pinus" & harv_st_clusters_subs$cluster_id == 23),]$Group   <- "Poland"


harv_st_clusters_subs[which(harv_st_clusters_subs$Genus == "otherBL" & harv_st_clusters_subs$cluster_id == 1 & harv_st_clusters_subs$Lon <= 0),]$Group   <- "UKIr"

harv_st_clusters_subs_groups <- harv_st_clusters_subs[which(!is.na(harv_st_clusters_subs$Group)),]

plot(harv_st_clusters_subs$Lon, harv_st_clusters_subs$Lat, col="light grey" )

points(harv_st_clusters_subs_groups[which(harv_st_clusters_subs_groups$Group =="CentralEuropeBL"),]$Lon, harv_st_clusters_subs_groups[which(harv_st_clusters_subs_groups$Group =="CentralEuropeBL"),]$Lat, col="magenta" ,pch=16)

points(harv_st_clusters_subs_groups[which(harv_st_clusters_subs_groups$Group =="CentralEuropeNL"),]$Lon, harv_st_clusters_subs_groups[which(harv_st_clusters_subs_groups$Group =="CentralEuropeNL"),]$Lat, col="brown" ,pch=16)

points(harv_st_clusters_subs_groups[which(harv_st_clusters_subs_groups$Group =="Poland"),]$Lon, harv_st_clusters_subs_groups[which(harv_st_clusters_subs_groups$Group =="Poland"),]$Lat, col="red" ,pch=16)

points(harv_st_clusters_subs_groups[which(harv_st_clusters_subs_groups$Group =="NorthernFinland"),]$Lon, harv_st_clusters_subs_groups[which(harv_st_clusters_subs_groups$Group =="NorthernFinland"),]$Lat, col="green" ,pch=16)

points(harv_st_clusters_subs_groups[which(harv_st_clusters_subs_groups$Group =="BalticsSouthernFinland"),]$Lon, harv_st_clusters_subs_groups[which(harv_st_clusters_subs_groups$Group =="BalticsSouthernFinland"),]$Lat, col="blue" ,pch=16)

points(harv_st_clusters_subs_groups[which(harv_st_clusters_subs_groups$Group =="EasternEurope"),]$Lon, harv_st_clusters_subs_groups[which(harv_st_clusters_subs_groups$Group =="EasternEurope"),]$Lat, col="purple" ,pch=16)

points(harv_st_clusters_subs_groups[which(harv_st_clusters_subs_groups$Group =="SouthernSweden"),]$Lon, harv_st_clusters_subs_groups[which(harv_st_clusters_subs_groups$Group =="SouthernSweden"),]$Lat, col="yellow" ,pch=16)

points(harv_st_clusters_subs_groups[which(harv_st_clusters_subs_groups$Group =="UKIr"),]$Lon, harv_st_clusters_subs_groups[which(harv_st_clusters_subs_groups$Group =="UKIr"),]$Lat, col="dark grey" ,pch=16)

#add colour to dataframe:
colour_key <- data.table(
  Group  = c("CentralEuropeBL", "CentralEuropeNL", "Poland", "NorthernFinland", 
             "BalticsSouthernFinland", "EasternEurope", "SouthernSweden", "UKIr"),
  colour = c("magenta", "brown", "red", "green", 
             "blue", "purple", "yellow", "dark grey")
)

harv_st_clusters_subs_groups[, colour := NA_character_]
harv_st_clusters_subs_groups[colour_key, on = "Group", colour := i.colour]


mtext(side=1, "Longitude",line=2)
mtext(side=2, "Latitude",line=2)

legend("topleft", 
       legend = colour_key$Group, 
       col    = colour_key$colour, 
       pch    = 16, 
       bty    = "n",
       cex=0.6)


```

```{r Management_impact_on_dist_cmass}

#investigate the impact of forest management on disturbance (risk?):

v16 <- "/Volumes/Anne's Backup/ForestPaths/v16/"  
base_dir <- v16
source("../../3_analysis_ForestPaths_Exploratory_runs/scripts/helper_functions.R")
management ="intensthin"
scenarios=c("ssp126","ssp370")
outdir <- "/Users/annemarie/Documents/1_TreeMort/2_Analysis/3_analysis_FORWARDS_restoration_scenarios/Disturbance_paper/Figures/"

# years for comparison:
earliest_period  <- c(2025,2050)
mid_period       <- c(2051,2070)
late_period      <- c(2071,2100)

landcover_fractions = read.table("../../3_analysis_ForestPaths_Exploratory_runs/landuse_change_forest_age/lu_inputfiles_noNF3/net_lu_HildaPucherMircaEurope.txt", head=TRUE)
landcover_fractions <- landcover_fractions[which(landcover_fractions$Year>=2025),]# subset, landcover constent from 2025.

pdf(paste0(outdir,"cmass_dist_effect.pdf"), width = 12, height = 6)

managementslist <- c("base" , "lightthin","intensthin"   ,"longrot"     , "shortrot"      ,"rettree",      "ccf",  "ceaseman", "ceaseman_cct","divers","ccf_divers")     #    "ceaseman_cct_distoff"

for( management in managementslist) {
  for(scenario in scenarios){
  

  #retrieve the source file location + add some metadata:
  folder_name_on <- paste0(base_dir,management, "_fut_MPI-ESM1-2-HR_",
                        scenario, "_diston")
  folder_name_off <- paste0(base_dir,management, "_fut_MPI-ESM1-2-HR_",
                        scenario, "_distoff")
  source.in_distoff = defineSource(id= paste0(management,"_",scenario),
                           dir= paste0(folder_name_off),
                           format = GUESS,
                           name = paste0(management,"_",scenario))
  
  source.in_diston = defineSource(id= paste0(management,"_",scenario),
                           dir= paste0(folder_name_on),
                           format = GUESS,
                           name = paste0(management,"_",scenario))
  
  
  # 1. Get forest biomass (from cmass_wood_sts or diam_cmass_wood)
  myData_cmass_distoff <- getField(source = source.in_distoff, quant = "cmass_wood_sts")
  myData_cmass_diston <- getField(source = source.in_diston, quant = "cmass_wood_sts")
  
  collect <- myData_cmass_diston@data
  collect <- collect[,c("Lon","Lat","Year")]
  collect$cmass_diff <- myData_cmass_diston@data$Forest_sum -  myData_cmass_distoff@data$Forest_sum

  # Define time period breaks; have the same period lengths: 
    collect[, Year := fcase(
       Year >= earliest_period[1] & Year <= earliest_period[2], as.numeric("2050"),
       Year > mid_period[1]       & Year <= mid_period[2], as.numeric("2070"),
       Year > late_period[1]      & Year <= late_period[2], as.numeric("2090")
     )]

    # Means by gridcell and period
    collect_cumsum <- collect[
      !is.na(Year),
      .(
        mean_cmass_diff = mean(cmass_diff, na.rm = TRUE)
        #FOREST    = first(FOREST)
      ),
      by = .(Lon, Lat, Year)
    ]
    
    #merge with the regions /(groups):
    
    collect_cumsum_merged <- collect_cumsum[harv_st_clusters_subs_groups[,c("Lon","Lat","Group","colour")], on = .(Lon, Lat)]
    collect_cumsum <- collect_cumsum_merged[which(!is.infinite(collect_cumsum_merged$mean_cmass_diff)),]
    myData_cmass_diston@data <- collect_cumsum
    plotSpatial(myData_cmass_diston, years=c(2050,2070,2090),layers="mean_cmass_diff", legend.title = "cmass diff")
    
    
    group_colours <- unique(collect_cumsum_merged[, .(Group, colour)])
    group_colours <- setNames(group_colours$colour, group_colours$Group)

    
   #plots the mean cumulative difference across all gridcells:
    p <- ggplot(collect_cumsum_merged, aes(x = Group, y = mean_cmass_diff, fill = Group)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "summary", fun = "mean") +
    stat_summary(fun.data = mean_se, fun.args = list(mult = 1), 
               geom = "errorbar", width = 0.4, colour = "black", alpha = 0.9) +
  
    scale_fill_manual(values = group_colours) +
    facet_wrap(~ Year, ncol = 3) +
    labs(x = NULL, y = "diston-distoff: 20 year mean (mean across region) [kgC/m2]", fill = "Group") +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
          legend.position = "bottom")
    p <- p + labs(title = paste(scenario, management))
    print(p)
    
    }
}
  
dev.off()



#plot by region:

# First collect all data
all_data <- list()

#collect data:
for( management in managementslist) {
  for(scenario in scenarios){
  

  #retrieve the source file location + add some metadata:
  folder_name_on <- paste0(base_dir,management, "_fut_MPI-ESM1-2-HR_",
                        scenario, "_diston")
  folder_name_off <- paste0(base_dir,management, "_fut_MPI-ESM1-2-HR_",
                        scenario, "_distoff")
  source.in_distoff = defineSource(id= paste0(management,"_",scenario),
                           dir= paste0(folder_name_off),
                           format = GUESS,
                           name = paste0(management,"_",scenario))
  
  source.in_diston = defineSource(id= paste0(management,"_",scenario),
                           dir= paste0(folder_name_on),
                           format = GUESS,
                           name = paste0(management,"_",scenario))
  
  
  # 1. Get forest biomass (from cmass_wood_sts or diam_cmass_wood)
  myData_cmass_distoff <- getField(source = source.in_distoff, quant = "cmass_wood_sts")
  myData_cmass_diston <- getField(source = source.in_diston, quant = "cmass_wood_sts")
  
  collect <- myData_cmass_diston@data
  collect <- collect[,c("Lon","Lat","Year")]
  collect$cmass_diff <- myData_cmass_diston@data$Forest_sum -  myData_cmass_distoff@data$Forest_sum
  collect$cmass_diston <- myData_cmass_diston@data$Forest_sum
  collect$cmass_distoff <-myData_cmass_distoff@data$Forest_sum
  
   # Define time period breaks; have the same period lengths: 
    collect[, Year := fcase(
       Year >= earliest_period[1] & Year <= earliest_period[2], as.numeric("2050"),
       Year > mid_period[1]       & Year <= mid_period[2], as.numeric("2070"),
       Year > late_period[1]      & Year <= late_period[2], as.numeric("2090")
     )]
    
    # sums by gridcell and period
    collect_sum <- collect[
      !is.na(Year),
      .(
        sum_cmass_diff = sum(cmass_diff, na.rm = TRUE),
        mean_cmass_diff = mean(cmass_diff, na.rm = TRUE),
        mean_cmass_diston = mean(cmass_diston, na.rm = TRUE),
        mean_cmass_distoff = mean(cmass_distoff, na.rm = TRUE)
        #FOREST    = first(FOREST)
      ),
      by = .(Lon, Lat, Year)
    ]
    
    #merge with the regions /(groups):
    
    collect_sum_merged <- collect_sum[harv_st_clusters_subs_groups[,c("Lon","Lat","Group","colour")], on = .(Lon, Lat)]
    
    # then tag it with the loop variables:
    collect_sum_merged[, scenario   := scenario]
    collect_sum_merged[, management := management]
    
    all_data[[paste(scenario, management, sep = "_")]] <- copy(collect_sum_merged)
  }
}

all_data_dt <- rbindlist(all_data)



pdf(paste0(outdir,"cmass_dist_effect_byregion.pdf"), width = 12, height = 6)

for(sc in scenarios){
  for(grp in unique(all_data_dt$Group)){
    
    plot_data <- all_data_dt[scenario == sc & Group == grp]
    
    mgmt_colours <- setNames(man$col, man$management)

    p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = management, y = mean_cmass_diff, fill = management)) +
      geom_bar(stat = "summary", fun = "mean") +
      stat_summary(fun.data = mean_se, fun.args = list(mult = 1), 
               geom = "errorbar", width = 0.4, colour = "black", alpha = 0.9) +
  
      scale_fill_manual(values = mgmt_colours) +
      facet_wrap(~ Year, ncol = 3) +
      labs(x = "Management", 
           y     = "cmass difference (diston - distoff), 20 year mean across region [KgC/m2]",
           fill  = "Management",
           title = paste(grp, sc)) +
      theme_bw() +
      theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
            legend.position = "bottom")
    print(p)
    
  }
}

dev.off()



pdf(paste0(outdir,"cmass_dist_effect_byregion_boxplots_distonoff.pdf"), width = 12, height = 6)

for(sc in scenarios){
  for(grp in unique(all_data_dt$Group)){
    
    plot_data <- all_data_dt[scenario == sc & Group == grp]
    
    mgmt_colours <- setNames(man$col, man$management)
    
    
    # -- Helper: lighten a colour by mixing with white (amount: 0=original, 1=white)
lighten_col <- function(col, amount = 0.55) {
  m <- col2rgb(col) / 255
  rgb(m[1,] + amount * (1 - m[1,]),
      m[2,] + amount * (1 - m[2,]),
      m[3,] + amount * (1 - m[3,]))
}

# -- Paired Wilcoxon test: diston vs distoff, per management × year
sig_df <- plot_data |>
  group_by(management,Year) |>
  summarise(
    p_val = tryCatch(
      wilcox.test(mean_cmass_diston, mean_cmass_distoff,
                  paired = TRUE, exact = FALSE)$p.value,
      error = function(e) NA_real_
    ),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    sig_label = case_when(
      is.na(p_val)  ~ "",
      p_val < 0.001 ~ "***",
      p_val < 0.01  ~ "**",
      p_val < 0.05  ~ "*",
      TRUE          ~ "ns"
    )
  )


# -- Pivot to long: one row per (management, year, dist_type)
plot_long <- plot_data |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = c(mean_cmass_diston, mean_cmass_distoff),
    names_to  = "dist_type",
    values_to = "mean_cmass"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    dist_type = factor(dist_type,
                       levels = c("mean_cmass_diston", "mean_cmass_distoff"),
                       labels = c("dist on", "dist off")),
    fill_id   = paste(management, dist_type, sep = "\u2014")
  )

# -- Y position for the label: just above the top whisker in each facet cell
sig_df <- plot_long |>
  group_by(management, Year) |>
  summarise(y_pos = max(mean_cmass, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
  left_join(sig_df, by = c("management", "Year"))


# -- Build fill map: full colour for "dist on", lightened for "dist off"
fill_map <- c(
  setNames(mgmt_colours,
           paste(names(mgmt_colours), "dist on",  sep = "\u2014")),
  setNames(lighten_col(mgmt_colours),
           paste(names(mgmt_colours), "dist off", sep = "\u2014"))
)

# -- Plot
p <- ggplot(plot_long,
            aes(x    = management,
                y    = mean_cmass,
                fill = fill_id)) +
  geom_boxplot(position = position_dodge(width = 0.8),
              width    = 0.9) +
  geom_text(data        = sig_df,
            aes(x = management, y = y_pos, label = sig_label),
            inherit.aes = FALSE,
            vjust       = -0.3,
            size        = 3) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = fill_map,
    name   = NULL,
    guide  = guide_legend(nrow = 2)
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ Year, ncol = 3) +
  labs(x     = "Management",
       y     = "cmass 20-year-mean across region [kgC/m\u00b2]",
       title = paste(unique(plot_data$Group), unique(plot_data$scenario))) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text.x     = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        legend.position = "bottom")

  print(p)
  }
}

dev.off()
```

```{r Management_impact_on_dist_NBP}

#investigate the impact of forest management on disturbance (risk?):

v16 <- "/Volumes/Anne's Backup/ForestPaths/v16/"  
base_dir <- v16
source("../../3_analysis_ForestPaths_Exploratory_runs/scripts/helper_functions.R")
management ="intensthin"
scenarios=c("ssp126","ssp370")

pdf(paste0(outdir,"nbp_dist_effect.pdf"), width = 12, height = 6)

for( management in managementslist) {
  for(scenario in scenarios){
  
  #retrieve the source file location + add some metadata:
  folder_name_on <- paste0(base_dir,management, "_fut_MPI-ESM1-2-HR_",
                        scenario, "_diston")
  folder_name_off <- paste0(base_dir,management, "_fut_MPI-ESM1-2-HR_",
                        scenario, "_distoff")
  source.in_distoff = defineSource(id= paste0(management,"_",scenario),
                           dir= paste0(folder_name_off),
                           format = GUESS,
                           name = paste0(management,"_",scenario))
  
  source.in_diston = defineSource(id= paste0(management,"_",scenario),
                           dir= paste0(folder_name_on),
                           format = GUESS,
                           name = paste0(management,"_",scenario))
  
  
  # 1. Get forest biomass (from cmass_wood_sts or diam_cmass_wood)
  # *-1.0
  myData_cmass_distoff <- getField(source = source.in_distoff, quant = "cflux_forest")
  myData_cmass_distoff@data$NBP <- (myData_cmass_distoff@data$NEE - myData_cmass_distoff@data$LU_ch)*-1.0
  myData_cmass_diston <- getField(source = source.in_diston, quant = "cflux_forest")
  myData_cmass_diston@data$NBP <- (myData_cmass_diston@data$NEE - myData_cmass_diston@data$LU_ch)*-1.0
  
  collect <- myData_cmass_diston@data
  collect <- collect[,c("Lon","Lat","Year")]
  collect$NBP_diff <- myData_cmass_diston@data$NBP -  myData_cmass_distoff@data$NBP
  
    myData_cmass_diston@data <- myData_cmass_diston@data[landcover_fractions[,c("Lon","Lat","Year","FOREST")], on = .(Lon, Lat, Year)]
    myData_cmass_diston@data$NBP_diff <-  myData_cmass_diston@data$NBP_diff /myData_cmass_diston@data$FOREST

    # Define time period breaks; have the same period lengths: 
    collect[, Year := fcase(
      Year >= earliest_period[1] & Year <= earliest_period[2], as.numeric("2050"),
      Year > mid_period[1]       & Year <= mid_period[2], as.numeric("2070"),
      Year > late_period[1]      & Year <= late_period[2], as.numeric("2090")
    )]
    
    # Cumulative sums by gridcell and period
    collect_cumsum <- collect[
      !is.na(Year),
      .(
        Cum_NBP_diff = mean(NBP_diff, na.rm = TRUE)
        
        #FOREST    = first(FOREST)
      ),
      by = .(Lon, Lat, Year)
    ]
    
    #merge with the regions /(groups):
    
    collect_cumsum_merged <- collect_cumsum[harv_st_clusters_subs_groups[,c("Lon","Lat","Group","colour")], on = .(Lon, Lat)]
    collect_cumsum <- collect_cumsum[which(!is.infinite(collect_cumsum$NBP_diff)),]
    #cmass_wood_lost@data <- cumsum_damage
    #plotSpatial(cmass_wood_lost, years=c(2050,2070,2090),layers="Cum_cmass_diff", legend.title = "cmas diff")
    
    
    group_colours <- unique(collect_cumsum_merged[, .(Group, colour)])
    group_colours <- setNames(group_colours$colour, group_colours$Group)

   #plots the mean cumulative difference across all gridcells:
    p <- ggplot(collect_cumsum_merged, aes(x = Group, y = Cum_NBP_diff, fill = Group)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "summary", fun = "mean") +
    stat_summary(fun.data = mean_se, fun.args = list(mult = 1), 
         geom = "errorbar", width = 0.4, colour = "black", alpha = 0.9) +
  
    scale_fill_manual(values = group_colours) +
    facet_wrap(~ Year, ncol = 3) +
    labs(x = NULL, y = "NBP difference (diston - distoff), 20 year mean across region [KgC/m2]", fill = "Group") +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
          legend.position = "bottom")
    p <- p + labs(title = paste(scenario, management))
    print(p)
    
    }
}
  
dev.off()



#plot by region:

# First collect all data
all_data <- list()

#collect data:
for( management in managementslist) {
  for(scenario in scenarios){
  
#retrieve the source file location + add some metadata:
  folder_name_on <- paste0(base_dir,management, "_fut_MPI-ESM1-2-HR_",
                        scenario, "_diston")
  folder_name_off <- paste0(base_dir,management, "_fut_MPI-ESM1-2-HR_",
                        scenario, "_distoff")
  source.in_distoff = defineSource(id= paste0(management,"_",scenario),
                           dir= paste0(folder_name_off),
                           format = GUESS,
                           name = paste0(management,"_",scenario))
  
  source.in_diston = defineSource(id= paste0(management,"_",scenario),
                           dir= paste0(folder_name_on),
                           format = GUESS,
                           name = paste0(management,"_",scenario))
  
  
  # 1. Get forest biomass (from cmass_wood_sts or diam_cmass_wood)
  myData_cmass_distoff <- getField(source = source.in_distoff, quant = "cflux_forest")
  myData_cmass_distoff@data$NBP <- (myData_cmass_distoff@data$NEE - myData_cmass_distoff@data$LU_ch )*-1.0
  myData_cmass_diston <- getField(source = source.in_diston, quant = "cflux_forest")
  myData_cmass_diston@data$NBP <- (myData_cmass_diston@data$NEE - myData_cmass_diston@data$LU_ch)*-1.0
  
  
  collect <- myData_cmass_diston@data
  collect <- collect[,c("Lon","Lat","Year")]
  collect$NBP_diff <- myData_cmass_diston@data$NBP -  myData_cmass_distoff@data$NBP
  collect$NBP_on  <- myData_cmass_diston@data$NBP 
  collect$NBP_off <- myData_cmass_distoff@data$NBP
  
  #calculate the MEAN cmass differences for timeperiods 2025-2050, 2050-2070, 2070-2100
  #FIRST, get to per forest area:
    myData_cmass_diston@data <- myData_cmass_diston@data[landcover_fractions[,c("Lon","Lat","Year","FOREST")], on = .(Lon, Lat, Year)]
    myData_cmass_diston@data$NBP <-  myData_cmass_diston@data$NBP /myData_cmass_diston@data$FOREST

  
    # Define time period breaks; have the same period lengths: 
    collect[, Year := fcase(
      Year >= earliest_period[1] & Year <= earliest_period[2], as.numeric("2050"),
      Year > mid_period[1]       & Year <= mid_period[2], as.numeric("2070"),
      Year > late_period[1]      & Year <= late_period[2], as.numeric("2090")
    )]
    
    # Cumulative sums by gridcell and period
    collect_cumsum <- collect[
      !is.na(Year),
      .(
        mean_NBP_diff = mean(NBP_diff, na.rm = TRUE),
        mean_NBP_on  = mean(NBP_on, na.rm = TRUE),
        mean_NBP_off = mean(NBP_off, na.rm = TRUE)
        #FOREST    = first(FOREST)
      ),
      by = .(Lon, Lat, Year)
    ]
    
    #merge with the regions /(groups):
    
    collect_cumsum_merged <- collect_cumsum[harv_st_clusters_subs_groups[,c("Lon","Lat","Group","colour")], on = .(Lon, Lat)]
    
    
    # then tag it with the loop variables:
    collect_cumsum_merged[, scenario   := scenario]
    collect_cumsum_merged[, management := management]
    
    all_data[[paste(scenario, management, sep = "_")]] <- copy(collect_cumsum_merged)
  }
}

all_data_dt <- rbindlist(all_data)



pdf(paste0(outdir,"nbp_dist_effect_byregion.pdf"), width = 12, height = 6)

for(sc in scenarios){
  for(grp in unique(all_data_dt$Group)){
    
    plot_data <- all_data_dt[scenario == sc & Group == grp]
    
    mgmt_colours <- setNames(man$col, man$management)

    p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = management, y = mean_NBP_diff, fill = management)) +
      geom_bar(stat = "summary", fun = "mean") +
       stat_summary(fun.data = mean_se, fun.args = list(mult = 1), 
         geom = "errorbar", width = 0.4, colour = "black", alpha = 0.9) +
  
      scale_fill_manual(values = mgmt_colours) +
      facet_wrap(~ Year, ncol = 3) +
      labs(x = "Management", 
           y     = "NBP difference (diston - distoff), 20 year mean across region [KgC/m2]",
           fill  = "Management",
           title = paste(grp, sc)) +
      theme_bw() +
      theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
            legend.position = "bottom")
    print(p)
  }
}

dev.off()




pdf(paste0(outdir,"nbp_byregion_boxplots_distonoff.pdf"), width = 12, height = 6)

for(sc in scenarios){
  for(grp in unique(all_data_dt$Group)){
    
    plot_data <- all_data_dt[scenario == sc & Group == grp]
    
    mgmt_colours <- setNames(man$col, man$management)
    
    
    # -- Helper: lighten a colour by mixing with white (amount: 0=original, 1=white)
lighten_col <- function(col, amount = 0.55) {
  m <- col2rgb(col) / 255
  rgb(m[1,] + amount * (1 - m[1,]),
      m[2,] + amount * (1 - m[2,]),
      m[3,] + amount * (1 - m[3,]))
}

# -- Paired Wilcoxon test: diston vs distoff, per management × year
sig_df <- plot_data |>
  group_by(management,Year) |>
  summarise(
    p_val = tryCatch(
      wilcox.test(mean_NBP_on, mean_NBP_off,
                  paired = TRUE, exact = FALSE)$p.value,
      error = function(e) NA_real_
    ),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    sig_label = case_when(
      is.na(p_val)  ~ "",
      p_val < 0.001 ~ "***",
      p_val < 0.01  ~ "**",
      p_val < 0.05  ~ "*",
      TRUE          ~ "ns"
    )
  )


# -- Pivot to long: one row per (management, year, dist_type)
plot_long <- plot_data |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = c(mean_NBP_on, mean_NBP_off),
    names_to  = "dist_type",
    values_to = "mean_NBP"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    dist_type = factor(dist_type,
                       levels = c("mean_NBP_on", "mean_NBP_off"),
                       labels = c("dist on", "dist off")),
    fill_id   = paste(management, dist_type, sep = "\u2014")
  )

# -- Y position for the label: just above the top whisker in each facet cell
sig_df <- plot_long |>
  group_by(management, Year) |>
  summarise(y_pos = max(mean_NBP, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
  left_join(sig_df, by = c("management", "Year"))


# -- Build fill map: full colour for "dist on", lightened for "dist off"
fill_map <- c(
  setNames(mgmt_colours,
           paste(names(mgmt_colours), "dist on",  sep = "\u2014")),
  setNames(lighten_col(mgmt_colours),
           paste(names(mgmt_colours), "dist off", sep = "\u2014"))
)

# -- Plot
p <- ggplot(plot_long,
            aes(x    = management,
                y    = mean_NBP,
                fill = fill_id)) +
  geom_boxplot(position = position_dodge(width = 0.8),
              width    = 0.9) +
  geom_text(data        = sig_df,
            aes(x = management, y = y_pos, label = sig_label),
            inherit.aes = FALSE,
            vjust       = -0.3,
            size        = 3) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = fill_map,
    name   = NULL,
    guide  = guide_legend(nrow = 2)
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ Year, ncol = 3) +
  labs(x     = "Management",
       y     = "NBP 20-year-mean across region [kgC/m\u00b2]",
       title = paste(unique(plot_data$Group), unique(plot_data$scenario))) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text.x     = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        legend.position = "bottom")

  print(p)
  }
}

dev.off()

```

```{r Management_impact_on_dist_harvest}

#investigate the impact of forest management on disturbance (risk?):

v16 <- "/Volumes/Anne's Backup/ForestPaths/v16/"  
base_dir <- v16
source("../../3_analysis_ForestPaths_Exploratory_runs/scripts/helper_functions.R")
management ="intensthin"
scenarios=c("ssp126","ssp370")

pdf(paste0(outdir,"harvest_dist_effect.pdf"), width = 12, height = 6)

for( management in managementslist) {
  for(scenario in scenarios){
  
  #retrieve the source file location + add some metadata:
  folder_name_on <- paste0(base_dir,management, "_fut_MPI-ESM1-2-HR_",
                        scenario, "_diston")
  folder_name_off <- paste0(base_dir,management, "_fut_MPI-ESM1-2-HR_",
                        scenario, "_distoff")
  source.in_distoff = defineSource(id= paste0(management,"_",scenario),
                           dir= paste0(folder_name_off),
                           format = GUESS,
                           name = paste0(management,"_",scenario))
  
  source.in_diston = defineSource(id= paste0(management,"_",scenario),
                           dir= paste0(folder_name_on),
                           format = GUESS,
                           name = paste0(management,"_",scenario))
  
  
  # 1. Get forest biomass (from cmass_wood_sts or diam_cmass_wood)
  myData_harv_thin_distoff <- getField(source = source.in_distoff, quant = "cmass_wood_harv_sts")
  #myData_harv_thin_distoff@data$Total <- rowSums(myData_harv_thin_distoff@data[,c(4:19)])
  #myData_harv_cct_distoff <- getField(source = source.in_distoff, quant = "closs_harv_ccut")
  #myData_harv_cct_distoff@data$Total <- rowSums(myData_harv_cct_distoff@data[,c(4:19)])
  #myData_harv_thin_distoff@data$TotHarv <- myData_harv_thin_distoff@data$Total +  myData_harv_cct_distoff@data$Total 
  
  myData_harv_thin_diston <- getField(source = source.in_diston, quant = "cmass_wood_harv_sts")
  #myData_harv_thin_diston@data$Total <- rowSums(myData_harv_thin_diston@data[,c(4:19)])
  #myData_harv_cct_diston <- getField(source = source.in_diston, quant = "closs_harv_ccut")
  #myData_harv_cct_diston@data$Total <- rowSums(myData_harv_cct_diston@data[,c(4:19)])
  #myData_harv_thin_diston@data$TotHarv <- myData_harv_thin_diston@data$Total +  myData_harv_cct_diston@data$Total 
  
  
  collect <- myData_harv_thin_diston@data          # was: myData_cmass_diston@data
  collect <- collect[, c("Lon", "Lat", "Year")]
  collect$TotHarv_diff <- myData_harv_thin_diston@data$Forest_sum -
                          myData_harv_thin_distoff@data$Forest_sum
   # Define time period breaks; have the same period lengths: 
    collect[, Year := fcase(
      Year >= earliest_period[1] & Year <= earliest_period[2], as.numeric("2050"),
      Year > mid_period[1]       & Year <= mid_period[2], as.numeric("2070"),
      Year > late_period[1]      & Year <= late_period[2], as.numeric("2090")
    )]
    
    # Cumulative sums by gridcell and period
    collect_cumsum <- collect[
      !is.na(Year),
      .(
        Cum_TotHarv_diff = mean(TotHarv_diff, na.rm = TRUE)
        #FOREST    = first(FOREST)
      ),
      by = .(Lon, Lat, Year)
    ]
    
    #merge with the regions /(groups):
    
    collect_cumsum_merged <- collect_cumsum[harv_st_clusters_subs_groups[,c("Lon","Lat","Group","colour")], on = .(Lon, Lat)]
    
    
    group_colours <- unique(collect_cumsum_merged[, .(Group, colour)])
    group_colours <- setNames(group_colours$colour, group_colours$Group)

   #plots the mean cumulative difference across all gridcells:
    p <- ggplot(collect_cumsum_merged, aes(x = Group, y = Cum_TotHarv_diff, fill = Group)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "summary", fun = "mean") +
      stat_summary(fun.data = mean_se, fun.args = list(mult = 1), 
         geom = "errorbar", width = 0.4, colour = "black", alpha = 0.9) +
  
    scale_fill_manual(values = group_colours) +
    facet_wrap(~ Year, ncol = 3) +
    labs(x = NULL, y = "harvest difference (diston - distoff), 20 year mean across region [KgC/m2]", fill = "Group") +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
          legend.position = "bottom")
    p <- p + labs(title = paste(scenario, management))
    print(p)
    
    }
}
  
dev.off()



#plot by region:

# First collect all data
all_data <- list()

#collect data:
for( management in managementslist) {
  for(scenario in scenarios){
  #retrieve the source file location + add some metadata:
  folder_name_on <- paste0(base_dir,management, "_fut_MPI-ESM1-2-HR_",
                        scenario, "_diston")
  folder_name_off <- paste0(base_dir,management, "_fut_MPI-ESM1-2-HR_",
                        scenario, "_distoff")
  source.in_distoff = defineSource(id= paste0(management,"_",scenario),
                           dir= paste0(folder_name_off),
                           format = GUESS,
                           name = paste0(management,"_",scenario))
  
  source.in_diston = defineSource(id= paste0(management,"_",scenario),
                           dir= paste0(folder_name_on),
                           format = GUESS,
                           name = paste0(management,"_",scenario))
  
  
  # 1. Get forest biomass (from cmass_wood_sts or diam_cmass_wood)
  myData_harv_thin_distoff <- getField(source = source.in_distoff, quant = "cmass_wood_harv_sts")
  myData_harv_thin_diston  <- getField(source = source.in_diston,  quant = "cmass_wood_harv_sts")

  
  collect <- myData_harv_thin_diston@data
  collect <- collect[,c("Lon","Lat","Year")]
  collect$TotHarv_diff <- myData_harv_thin_diston@data$Forest_sum -  myData_harv_thin_distoff@data$Forest_sum
  collect$TotHarv_on   <- myData_harv_thin_diston@data$Forest_sum 
  collect$TotHarv_off  <- myData_harv_thin_distoff@data$Forest_sum


    # Define time period breaks; have the same period lengths: 
    collect[, Year := fcase(
      Year >= earliest_period[1] & Year <= earliest_period[2], as.numeric("2050"),
      Year > mid_period[1]       & Year <= mid_period[2], as.numeric("2070"),
      Year > late_period[1]      & Year <= late_period[2], as.numeric("2090")
    )]
    
    # Mean: by gridcell and period; forest-area-weighted mean
    collect_cumsum <- collect[
      !is.na(Year),
      .(
        mean_TotHarv_diff = mean(TotHarv_diff, na.rm = TRUE),
        mean_TotHarv_on   = mean(TotHarv_on, na.rm = TRUE),
        mean_TotHarv_off  = mean(TotHarv_off, na.rm = TRUE),

        #FOREST    = first(FOREST)
      ),
      by = .(Lon, Lat, Year)
    ]
    

    
    #merge with the regions /(groups):
    
    collect_cumsum_merged <- collect_cumsum[harv_st_clusters_subs_groups[,c("Lon","Lat","Group","colour")], on = .(Lon, Lat)]
    
    # then tag it with the loop variables:
    collect_cumsum_merged[, scenario   := scenario]
    collect_cumsum_merged[, management := management]
    
    all_data[[paste(scenario, management, sep = "_")]] <- copy(collect_cumsum_merged)
  }
}

all_data_dt <- rbindlist(all_data)

pdf(paste0(outdir, "harvest_dist_effect_byregion.pdf"), width = 12, height = 6)
for (sc in scenarios) {
  for (grp in unique(all_data_dt$Group)) {
    plot_data <- all_data_dt[scenario == sc & Group == grp]
    mgmt_colours <- setNames(man$col, man$management)
    p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = management, y = mean_TotHarv_diff, fill = management)) +
      geom_bar(stat = "summary", fun = "mean") +
      stat_summary(fun.data = mean_se, fun.args = list(mult = 1),
                   geom = "errorbar", width = 0.4, colour = "black", alpha = 0.9) +
      scale_fill_manual(values = mgmt_colours) +
      facet_wrap(~ Year, ncol = 3) +
      labs(x = "Management",
           y     = "Harvest difference (diston - distoff), 20-year mean across region [kgC/m\u00b2]",
           fill  = "Management",
           title = paste(grp, sc)) +
      theme_bw() +
      theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
            legend.position = "bottom")
    print(p)
  }
}
dev.off() 
pdf(paste0(outdir,"harvest_byregion_boxplots_distonoff.pdf"), width = 12, height = 6)

for(sc in scenarios){
  for(grp in unique(all_data_dt$Group)){
    
    plot_data <- all_data_dt[scenario == sc & Group == grp]
    
    mgmt_colours <- setNames(man$col, man$management)
    
    
    # -- Helper: lighten a colour by mixing with white (amount: 0=original, 1=white)
lighten_col <- function(col, amount = 0.55) {
  m <- col2rgb(col) / 255
  rgb(m[1,] + amount * (1 - m[1,]),
      m[2,] + amount * (1 - m[2,]),
      m[3,] + amount * (1 - m[3,]))
}

# -- Paired Wilcoxon test: diston vs distoff, per management × year
sig_df <- plot_data |>
  group_by(management,Year) |>
  summarise(
    p_val = tryCatch(
      wilcox.test(mean_TotHarv_on, mean_TotHarv_off,
                  paired = TRUE, exact = FALSE)$p.value,
      error = function(e) NA_real_
    ),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    sig_label = case_when(
      is.na(p_val)  ~ "",
      p_val < 0.001 ~ "***",
      p_val < 0.01  ~ "**",
      p_val < 0.05  ~ "*",
      TRUE          ~ "ns"
    )
  )


# -- Pivot to long: one row per (management, year, dist_type)
plot_long <- plot_data |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = c(mean_TotHarv_on, mean_TotHarv_off),
    names_to  = "dist_type",
    values_to = "mean_harvest"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    dist_type = factor(dist_type,
                       levels = c("mean_TotHarv_on", "mean_TotHarv_off"),
                       labels = c("dist on", "dist off")),
    fill_id   = paste(management, dist_type, sep = "\u2014")
  )

# -- Y position for the label: just above the top whisker in each facet cell
sig_df <- plot_long |>
  group_by(management, Year) |>
  summarise(y_pos = max(mean_harvest, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
  left_join(sig_df, by = c("management", "Year"))


# -- Build fill map: full colour for "dist on", lightened for "dist off"
fill_map <- c(
  setNames(mgmt_colours,
           paste(names(mgmt_colours), "dist on",  sep = "\u2014")),
  setNames(lighten_col(mgmt_colours),
           paste(names(mgmt_colours), "dist off", sep = "\u2014"))
)

# -- Plot
p <- ggplot(plot_long,
            aes(x    = management,
                y    = mean_harvest,
                fill = fill_id)) +
  geom_boxplot(position = position_dodge(width = 0.8),
              width    = 0.9) +
  geom_text(data        = sig_df,
            aes(x = management, y = y_pos, label = sig_label),
            inherit.aes = FALSE,
            vjust       = -0.3,
            size        = 3) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = fill_map,
    name   = NULL,
    guide  = guide_legend(nrow = 2)
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ Year, ncol = 3) +
  labs(x     = "Management",
       y     = "Harvest 20-year-mean across region [kgC/m\u00b2]",
       title = paste(unique(plot_data$Group), unique(plot_data$scenario))) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text.x     = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        legend.position = "bottom")

  print(p)
  }
}

dev.off()


```

```{r Management_impact_on_disturbance_losses_cumulative_disturbance_plots}


# First collect all data
all_data <- list()

#collect data:
for( management in managementslist) {
  for(scenario in c("ssp370","ssp126")){
   
    folder_name <- paste0(base_dir,management, "_fut_MPI-ESM1-2-HR_",
                                scenario, "_diston")
    
    #retrieve the source file location + add some metadata:
    source.in = defineSource(id= paste0(management,"_",scenario),
                                     dir= paste0(folder_name),
                                     format = GUESS,
                                     name = paste0(management,"_",scenario))
            
            
    # ============================================================
    # 1. TOTAL FOREST BIOMASS (from diam_cmass_wood or cmass_wood_sts)
    # ============================================================
    
    myData_cmass <- getField(source = source.in, quant = "diam_cmass_wood")
    myData_cmass@data$Total <- rowSums(myData_cmass@data[, 4:dim(myData_cmass@data)[2]])
    myData_cmass@data <- merge(myData_cmass@data, frac_lu, by = c("Lon", "Lat", "Year"))
    
    # Calculate gridcell area
    myData_cmass@data[, GridcellArea_m2 := GridcellArea_m2(Lat, res = 0.5)]
    
    # Total is in kgC/m² of gridcell (already weighted by all land fractions)
    # To get absolute kgC in forest: multiply by gridcell area
    # But diam_cmass_wood includes all landcover, so we need forest fraction
    # Actually: Total * FOREST gives forest contribution per m² gridcell
    # Then multiply by gridcell area to get absolute kgC
    
    merged_biomass <- myData_cmass@data[, Forest_biomass_kgC := Total * FOREST * GridcellArea_m2]
    
    # Sum across all gridcells per year
    biomass_EU <- myData_cmass@data[, .(EU_forest_biomass_kgC = sum(Forest_biomass_kgC, na.rm = TRUE)), by = Year]
    
    # ============================================================
    # 2. STORM DAMAGE (from storm.out --> DamWoodC)
    # ============================================================
    
    myData_storm <- getField(source = source.in, quant = "storm")
    merged_storm <- myData_storm@data[frac_lu, on = .(Lon, Lat, Year)]
    
    # DamWoodC is already kgC/m² of gridcell (forest contribution)
    # Multiply by gridcell area to get absolute kgC
    merged_storm[, GridcellArea_m2 := GridcellArea_m2(Lat, res = 0.5)]
    merged_storm[, Storm_damage_kgC := DamWoodC * GridcellArea_m2]
    
    
    # Sum across all gridcells per year
    storm_EU <- merged_storm[, .(EU_storm_damage_kgC = sum(Storm_damage_kgC, na.rm = TRUE)), by = Year]
    
    # ============================================================
    # 3. INSECT DAMAGE (from storm.out -> InsDWC)
    # ============================================================
    
    myData_insect <- getField(source = source.in, quant = "storm")
    merged_insect <- myData_insect@data[frac_lu, on = .(Lon, Lat, Year)]
    
    # InsDWC is already kgC/m² of gridcell (forest contribution)
    # Multiply by gridcell area to get absolute kgC
    merged_insect[, GridcellArea_m2 := GridcellArea_m2(Lat, res = 0.5)]
    merged_insect[, Insect_damage_kgC := InsDWC * GridcellArea_m2]
    
    # Sum across all gridcells per year
    insect_EU <- merged_insect[, .(EU_insect_damage_kgC = sum(Insect_damage_kgC, na.rm = TRUE)), by = Year]
    
    # ============================================================
    # 4. FIRE LOSSES (from cflux_forest.out)
    # ============================================================
    # note that cflux_forest contains all combusted carbon, whereas the other variables only contain wood.
    # but the difference is probably not that large.
    myData_fire <- getField(source = source.in, quant = "cflux_forest")
    
    # Fire column is already in kgC/m² of gridcell (forest fire contribution)
    # Multiply by gridcell area to get absolute kgC
    myData_fire@data[, GridcellArea_m2 := GridcellArea_m2(Lat, res = 0.5)]
    myData_fire@data[, Fire_damage_kgC := Fire * GridcellArea_m2]
    
    merged_fire <- myData_fire@data[, Fire_damage_kgC := Fire * GridcellArea_m2]
    
    # Sum across all gridcells per year
    fire_EU <- myData_fire@data[, .(EU_fire_damage_kgC = sum(Fire_damage_kgC, na.rm = TRUE)), by = Year]
  
    
    # collect all variables
    collect <- merged_fire[,c("Lon","Lat","Year","Fire_damage_kgC")]
    collect <- collect[merged_insect[,c("Lon","Lat","Year","Insect_damage_kgC")],on =.(Lon,Lat,Year)]
    collect <- collect[merged_storm[,c("Lon","Lat","Year","Storm_damage_kgC")],on =.(Lon,Lat,Year)]
    collect <- collect[merged_biomass[,c("Lon","Lat","Year","Forest_biomass_kgC")],on =.(Lon,Lat,Year)]
    
    #remove 2024:
    collect <- collect[which(collect$Year>2024),]
    
    # Define time period breaks; have the same period lengths: 
    collect[, Year := fcase(
      Year >= earliest_period[1] & Year <= earliest_period[2], as.numeric("2050"),
      Year > mid_period[1]       & Year <= mid_period[2], as.numeric("2070"),
      Year > late_period[1]      & Year <= late_period[2], as.numeric("2090")
    )]
    
    # sums or means by gridcell and period and change unit to MtC:
    collect_sum <- collect[
      !is.na(Year),
      .(
        Fire_damage_kgC = mean(Fire_damage_kgC, na.rm = TRUE)/1000, #KgC to MtC,
        Insect_damage_kgC = mean(Insect_damage_kgC, na.rm = TRUE)/1000, #KgC to MtC,
        Storm_damage_kgC = mean(Storm_damage_kgC, na.rm = TRUE)/1000, #KgC to MtC,
        Forest_biomass_kgC = mean(Forest_biomass_kgC, na.rm = TRUE)/1000 #KgC to MtC
        #FOREST    = first(FOREST)
      ),
      by = .(Lon, Lat, Year)
    ]
    
    # actually, change to % biomass present, to not have y-axis allover the place across Groups/regions:
    collect_sum[, storm_perc_lost := Storm_damage_kgC / Forest_biomass_kgC * 100]
    collect_sum[, insect_perc_lost := Insect_damage_kgC / Forest_biomass_kgC * 100]
    collect_sum[, fire_perc_lost := Fire_damage_kgC / Forest_biomass_kgC * 100]
    
    #merge with the regions /(groups):
    
    collect_sum_merged <- collect_sum[harv_st_clusters_subs_groups[,c("Lon","Lat","Group","colour")], on = .(Lon, Lat)]
    
    # then tag it with the loop variables:
    collect_sum_merged[, scenario   := scenario]
    collect_sum_merged[, management := management]
    
    all_data[[paste(scenario, management, sep = "_")]] <- copy(collect_sum_merged)
  }
}

all_data_dt <- rbindlist(all_data)



# ── Adaptive page height: 2.5 in per group + space for title/legend ───────────
n_groups    <- length(unique(all_data_dt$Group))
page_height <- 2.5 * n_groups + 2

pdf(paste0(outdir, "disturbance_losses_byregion_and_period_barplot.pdf"),
    width = 13, height = page_height)

for (sc in scenarios) {

  sc_data <- all_data_dt[scenario == sc]
  if (nrow(sc_data) == 0L) next

  # ── Long format for all groups at once ───────────────────────────────────────
  plot_long_all <- data.table::melt(
    sc_data,
    measure.vars  = c("insect_perc_lost", "storm_perc_lost"),
    variable.name = "damage_type",
    value.name    = "damage_perc"
  )
  plot_long_all[, damage_type := factor(damage_type,
    levels = c("insect_perc_lost", "storm_perc_lost"),
    labels = c("Insect", "Storm"))]
  plot_long_all[, Year    := factor(Year, levels = year_levels)]
  plot_long_all[, fill_id := paste(management, as.character(Year), sep = "_")]
  plot_long_all[, Group   := factor(Group, levels = unique(all_data_dt$Group))]

  # ── Helper: one damage-type column ───────────────────────────────────────────
  # show_group_labels: TRUE only for the rightmost column (storm) to avoid
  # repeating group names; the aligned rows make them readable from one side.
  make_col <- function(dt, ylab, show_group_labels = FALSE) {
    ggplot(dt, aes(x = management, y = damage_perc, fill = fill_id)) +
      geom_bar(stat     = "summary",
               fun      = "mean",
               position = position_dodge(width = 0.9),
               width    = 0.9) +
      stat_summary(fun.data = mean_se,
                   geom     = "errorbar",
                   width    = 0.15,
                   colour   = "black",
                   position = position_dodge(width = 0.9)) +
      scale_fill_manual(values = fill_map,
                        breaks = legend_breaks,
                        labels = legend_labels,
                        name   = "Year") +
      facet_wrap(~ Group, ncol = 1, strip.position = "right",
                 scales = "free_y") +      # ← free per group panel
      labs(x = NULL, y = ylab) +
      theme_bw() +
      theme(
        axis.text.x      = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        strip.text.y     = if (show_group_labels)
                             element_text(size = 9, face = "bold", angle = 0)
                           else
                             element_blank(),
        strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey93", colour = NA)
      )
  }

  p_insect <- make_col(
    plot_long_all[damage_type == "Insect"],
    "Insect damage [% Biomass]",
    show_group_labels = FALSE
  )

  p_storm <- make_col(
    plot_long_all[damage_type == "Storm"],
   
    "Storm damage [% Biomass]",
    show_group_labels = TRUE   # group names on right edge of page
  )

  # ── Extract shared legend, then suppress on both plots ───────────────────────
  .get_legend <- function(p) {
    g   <- ggplot2::ggplotGrob(p + theme(legend.position = "right"))
    idx <- which(vapply(g$grobs, function(x) x$name, character(1)) == "guide-box")
    if (length(idx) == 0L) return(NULL)
    g$grobs[[idx[1]]]
  }

  leg            <- .get_legend(p_insect)
  p_insect_noleg <- p_insect + theme(legend.position = "none")
  p_storm_noleg  <- p_storm  + theme(legend.position = "none")

  # ── Assemble: title / two columns / legend ───────────────────────────────────
  title_grob <- grid::textGrob(
    paste("Disturbance damage \u2014", sc),
    gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 12, fontface = "bold")
  )

  body_grob <- gridExtra::arrangeGrob(
    p_insect_noleg, p_storm_noleg,
    nrow   = 1,
    widths = grid::unit(c(1, 1), "null")
  )

  combined <- if (!is.null(leg)) {
    gridExtra::arrangeGrob(
      title_grob, body_grob, leg,
      ncol    = 1,
      heights = grid::unit(c(0.04, 1, 0.06), c("npc", "null", "npc"))
    )
  } else {
    gridExtra::arrangeGrob(
      title_grob, body_grob,
      ncol    = 1,
      heights = grid::unit(c(0.04, 1), c("npc", "null"))
    )
  }

  grid::grid.draw(combined)
  grid::grid.newpage()   # advance to next page before next iteration
}

dev.off()



```
